channel.title: Awesome Search KG
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Awesome Search KG

item.title: Doug Turnbull
item.description: Doug Turnbull Co-founder of OpenSource Connections (OSC); co-author of Relevant Search (O’Reilly).

item.title: All about Information Retrieval & Search
item.description: Awesome Search — Information Retrieval & Search Knowledge Graph Hello, I am Andrew.

item.title: Judgment Lists
item.description: Judgment Lists Definition A judgment list (also called “qrels” in IR — short for “query relevance”) is a dataset of (query, document, relevance_grade) triples created by human annotators.

item.title: Vector Search Evaluation
item.description: Vector Search Evaluation Definition Vector search evaluation is the practice of measuring the relevance quality of Dense Vector Retrieval (semantic, multimodal, hybrid) using the same IR machinery as lexical search — Judgment Lists and metrics like NDCG, MRR, and Precision and Recall — but adapted t...

item.title: History
item.description: Awesome Search — Note History Chronological log of notes added to this knowledge graph. Newest first.

item.title: Index
item.description: Awesome Search — Information Retrieval & Search Knowledge Graph Hello, I am Andrew.

item.title: global_toc
item.description: Global Table of Contents A map of every note in the vault organized by category and theme.

item.title: Creating Judgement Lists with Quepid
item.description: End-to-end guide to building collaborative relevance judgement lists in Quepid — cases, teams, books of judgements, explicit information needs, scorers, and the human rating workflow.

item.title: How to Evaluate Image Search in Qdrant Using Quepid Part 1 (No Hacks)
item.description: Part 1 of evaluating image search: data preparation. Building a CLIP-based cross-modal image search over Amazon shopping products in Qdrant, before wiring up evaluation.

item.title: How to Evaluate Image Search in Qdrant Using Quepid Part 2 (Hacks)
item.description: Part 2: wiring Qdrant into Quepid as a custom search endpoint via an unofficial API, injecting CLIP query vectors through query options, and the hacks (and a PR) needed to render image results in the rating UI.
